Bug / Task Description: Percent-decoding mishandles a truncated escape at the very end of the input. When a '%' is followed by fewer than two remaining characters, the decoder reads past the end of the string instead of reporting a short-escape error, causing a panic or wrong output. A percent escape requires two hex digits to follow. Fix the bounds check so a percent sign without two following characters is reported as a short escape rather than read out of range.
Commit Subject: fix(uri): reject percent-escapes that lack two hex digits
